title: Explicit trust base for the PNP self-verification stack
purpose: List the assumptions that remain outside the repository's own executable acceptance result and bind each assumption to the files that represent or reduce it.

assumptions.title: JavaScript runtime correctness for the checked fragment
assumptions.description: The Node.js runtime correctly executes the ES module fragment used by the repository checkers, including deterministic JSON parsing, file IO, process exit codes, array/object semantics, and integer-safe arithmetic as guarded by the checkers.
assumptions.reductionPlan: Add second-runtime replay for more checker families and bounded integer validation at checker boundaries.

assumptions.title: SHA256 collision resistance for artifact identity
assumptions.description: SHA256 digests used as artifact identities, cache keys, and checksum ledgers are assumed collision resistant. Digest equality is not treated as semantic equality unless followed by canonical-byte or full-key comparison where required.
assumptions.reductionPlan: Add independent SHA256 implementation comparison and canonical-byte replay checks across runtimes.

assumptions.title: Git object integrity and checkout fidelity
assumptions.description: The checkout being verified is assumed to faithfully represent the intended Git object graph and file contents for the commit under review.
assumptions.reductionPlan: Add fresh-clone verification and detached commit/hash manifest checks.

assumptions.title: Formal semantics of NAND direct-wire words
assumptions.description: The mathematical interpretation of NAND direct-wire syntax, open carriers, boundary tuples, output tuples, evaluation, replacement, and size is assumed to match the checker-visible semantics used by the proof stack.
assumptions.reductionPlan: Materialize an executable NAND direct-wire semantics spec and exhaustive small-model tests.

assumptions.title: SAT-to-locked-NAND encoding theorem
assumptions.description: The locked NAND construction is assumed to correctly encode SAT into the stated exact-minimum threshold condition over residual slack.
assumptions.reductionPlan: Add SAT encoding truth-table verification for small CNF/NAND instances and a dedicated locked-NAND semantics ledger.

assumptions.title: PCC checker inference rules and proof-kernel soundness
assumptions.description: The PCC-K primitive rules, Sigma registry, reflection registry, proof-DAG discipline, and checker implementations are assumed sound for the proof objects they accept.
assumptions.reductionPlan: Add checker totality fuzzing, negative mutation generation, and independent replay of a core checker subset.

assumptions.title: Complexity implication SAT in P implies P equals NP
assumptions.description: The standard complexity-theory implication from a polynomial-time SAT decision procedure to P = NP is assumed, including the NP-completeness of SAT and the polynomial-time reductions defining NP-completeness.
currentMitigations: status boundary preventing public theorem emission
assumptions.reductionPlan: Create a machine-readable complexity ledger binding SAT is NP-complete, SAT in P, and P = NP as explicit proof objects.
